docs.title: How I Solve Problems (Same Process, Any Industry)
docs.text:
I don't just solve one-off issues. I bring a battle-tested process that works whether you're in retail, logistics, SaaS, or manufacturing—because good ML engineering transfers across domains.

1. Find the Money Leak

I dig into your KPIs, workflows, and constraints to pinpoint where you're bleeding margin—no generic playbooks.

2. Honest Options (Not Just ML)

You get 2–3 solutions—simple rules, heuristics, or ML—with estimated ROI for each. I'll tell you if ML isn't worth it.

3. The Business Case First

Before writing code, you receive an Optimization Report: impact projections, roadmap, and risks. No surprises later.

4. Production-Grade Delivery

End-to-end MLOps pipelines—automated, monitored, and scalable. Not "works on my laptop." Built to run without you.

docs.title: About Daksh Vanshaj
docs.text:
I'm a Data Scientist & MLOps Engineer who builds ML systems that actually make money—not just impressive demos that never leave the lab.

At 22, I've learned one hard truth: most ML projects fail not because of bad models, but because they don't solve real business problems. That's why I start with ROI, not algorithms.

docs.title: My Core Philosophy
docs.text:
Machine Learning is only valuable when it cuts costs or boosts revenue. I don't build "models in a vacuum"—I build production systems that deliver measurable business impact:

🚀 ROI-Driven

Every project starts with a feasibility report showing estimated impact in dollars. No guessing. No "let's train a model and see what happens."

⚡ Modern & Lean

I use high-performance, cost-effective tools (Polars, DuckDB) over bloated legacy stacks. Faster pipelines = lower cloud bills = higher margins.

🛡️ Production-Ready

Automated testing, drift monitoring, CI/CD pipelines—non-negotiable. Your system runs reliably without constant firefighting.

docs.title: What I Bring to Your Business
docs.text:
Data Engineering

Polars, DuckDB, PySpark, Delta Lake, DVC—I build pipelines that handle millions of rows without breaking your budget.

Machine Learning

XGBoost, Scikit-learn, Optuna, SHAP—I pick the right algorithm for your problem, not the trendiest one.

MLOps

MLflow, BentoML, FastAPI, Evidently AI, Kubernetes—your models deploy once and run forever (with monitoring so you know when they break).

Business Translation

ROI modeling, feasibility reports, stakeholder communication—I speak both Python and profit margins.

docs.title: How I Work with You
docs.text:
I don't claim to know your industry better than you do. Here's my honest process:

1. I Listen

You explain the problem. I ask about constraints, KPIs, and what you've already tried.

2. I Research

I study your domain, explore 2-3 solution approaches (ML, heuristics, or simple automation), and assess feasibility.

3. I Prove It First

Before writing production code, you get a feasibility report with rough ROI estimates. No surprises later.

4. I Build & Deliver

End-to-end MLOps pipeline—automated, monitored, documented. You own it, not me.

Industry Agnostic

This process means I can tackle retail, logistics, SaaS, or manufacturing—because the process transfers, even when the domain changes.

View My GitHub Get In Touch

docs.title: What Happens on the Call?
docs.text:
I Listen

You walk me through your business problem, constraints, and what success looks like.

I Dig Deeper

I'll ask about your data, KPIs, and what you've already tried (so we don't repeat failed experiments).

I Research

After we talk, I spend time researching 2-3 potential solutions tailored to your situation.

You Get a Free Mini-Report

Within 48 hours, I send you a short feasibility memo with rough ROI estimates, technical approach, and honest advice—even if ML isn't the answer.

You Decide

No pressure. If it makes sense to work together, great. If not, you still walk away with clarity.

docs.title: Common Questions
docs.text:
"What if my problem is in a niche or weird industry?"

That's where I do my best work. My process is built to handle domain complexity through deep research. Retail, manufacturing, SaaS, logistics—same rigorous method, different data. If you have a margin problem and data, I can probably help.

"Do I need a huge data infrastructure to start?"

Nope. Many high-ROI projects start with basic CSV files and spreadsheets. I'll work with what you have and build scalable systems as you grow. You don't need a data lake to get 20% cost savings.

"How much does this cost?"

The discovery call is free. If we decide to work together, I'll send a custom proposal based on scope—typically project-based pricing with clear deliverables and milestones. No surprises.

"What if we don't have a data team?"

Perfect. I build solutions you can own and maintain without hiring ML engineers. Full documentation, training, and handoff—so you're not dependent on me forever.

docs.title: How I Work: My 5-Step Profit Engine
docs.text:
1. Find the Money Leak

We identify where you're bleeding margin—stockouts, churn, inefficient ops, or bad pricing. I listen to your workflows and define success in dollars, not just model accuracy.

2. Research What Actually Works

I don't force ML where it doesn't belong. I research best-fit solutions (ML, rules-based, or simple heuristics) and give you 2-3 options with estimated ROI—so you choose what fits your risk appetite.

3. The Business Case (Before We Build)

You get a clear Optimization Report: impact projections, technical roadmap, and timeline. No surprises. No "trust me" engineering. We're aligned on value before I write a line of code.

4. Build Production-Ready Systems

I deliver end-to-end MLOps pipelines—automated data cleaning, model training, drift monitoring, CI/CD integration. Not just notebooks. Your solution runs reliably without babysitting.

5. Handover + Real-Time Tracking

I don't "deploy and disappear." You get documentation, team training, and live dashboards showing ROI in real-time. You'll see the impact—and own the system.

docs.title: Proof of Impact
docs.text: I don't just talk about what I can do—I show you exactly how I've done it. My work isn't just a collection of tech demos; it's Proof of how I turn business problems into measurable profit.

docs.title: Featured: StoreCast
docs.text:
StoreCast: Production-Grade Forecasting System

Self-Initiated Project

The Problem:

Using real retail data, I simulated a 45-store chain bleeding margin from an 11.85% forecasting error—resulting in $216M trapped in safety stock, constant stockouts, and operational chaos.

The Solution:

Built a production-ready MLOps pipeline (Polars, DuckDB, XGBoost) that slashed forecast error to 7.76%—with automated retraining, drift monitoring, and real-time dashboards.

The Impact:
  • $9.1M in projected annual savings (reduced stockouts and overstock waste)
  • $17.3M in freed working capital (35% less safety stock needed)
  • 30% reduction in operational inefficiency

What This Proves: This project demonstrates the exact methodology I bring to client engagements—from feasibility research to production deployment using real-world messy data.

Read the Technical Breakdown →

docs.title: How I'd save a 45-store retail chain $9.1M using real transaction data and my 5-step consulting process
docs.text:
Project Type: Self-initiated demonstration using real retail data Goal: Prove I can build production-ready ML systems that deliver measurable ROI Context: Simulated a consulting engagement for a retail chain bleeding margin from forecast errors

View Full Technical Breakdown → View Source Code on GitHub →

docs.title: The Business Problem (Simulated Context)
docs.text:
Using real transaction data from a 45-store retail region generating $2.45 Billion in annualized revenue, I analyzed a classic profitability crisis:

The Pain Points
  • Forecasting Chaos: Manual heuristics producing an 11.85% error—leading to stockouts and massive overstock.
  • Trapped Capital: $216M in safety stock sitting idle just to buffer against unpredictability.
  • Wasted Markdowns: $110M burned on blanket discounts without regional elasticity modeling.
The Dollar Impact

This level of forecasting error would cost the business $9.1M annually in operational waste and tie up $17.3M in working capital that could be deployed elsewhere.

docs.title: My 5-Step Process (How I Approached It)
docs.text:
This project showcases my repeatable methodology—the exact process I'd bring to your business challenge:

1. Problem Discovery

I analyzed the supply chain constraints, inventory turnover targets, and margin requirements. Built a simple ROI model connecting forecast error to hard dollar losses.

2. Research & Solution Evaluation

Evaluated 3 approaches: Simple statistical heuristics, Random Forest, and XGBoost (chosen for best accuracy-to-complexity ratio).

3. Feasibility Report (Before Building)

Mapped mathematical error reduction (11.85% → ~8%) to projected business impact ($9.1M savings). Stakeholders align on value before production code starts.

4. Production-Grade Build

Implemented a Medallion Lakehouse architecture using Polars (10x faster cleaning) and DuckDB (SQL aggregations without cluster overhead).

5. MLOps Deployment

Built a Human-in-the-Loop pipeline with MLflow for tracking, Optuna for tuning, SHAP for explainability, and Evidently AI for drift detection.

docs.title: Why This Stack?Key Engineering DecisionsMLOps Rigor
docs.text:
• Rejected: Legacy tools like Hadoop/Pandas that bloat cloud costs.
  • Chose: Modern engines (Polars, DuckDB) that deliver 10x speed at 60% lower memory footprint.
  • Polars over Pandas: Rust-backed parallelism cut processing from 2 hours → 5 minutes.
  • DuckDB over Spark: In-process SQL perfect for 1M rows—no cluster overhead.
  • XGBoost over Deep Learning: Simpler, explainable, and production-proven.
  • Automated: Weekly retraining + drift-triggered updates.
  • Monitored: Real-time dashboards for accuracy, drift, and ROI.
  • Audit Trail: Full experiment logging via MLflow.

docs.title: Results & Impact (Projected)$9.1M$17.3M320 hrs
docs.text:
Annual Profit Increase

Freed Working Capital

Saved/Month via Automation

docs.title: Operational Wins:
docs.text:
• Zero manual intervention after deployment.
  • Drift detection caught seasonal shifts 2 weeks before accuracy degraded.
  • Full explainability via SHAP—stakeholders understand why forecasts changed.

docs.title: What This Proves About My Process
docs.text:
• I Think ROI-First: Started with dollar impact modeling, not algorithm selection.
  • I Handle Real-World Complexity: 1M+ transactions, missing data, and seasonality—not a clean lab dataset.
  • I Build Systems, Not Notebooks: Production-grade infrastructure that runs reliably.
  • I Don't Overengineer: Chose the 80/20 solution (XGBoost + DuckDB) to maximize speed-to-value.
